name: Umm Ishaq
title: Loyal Wife
shortDescription: Wife of Imam Hussain (AS) and mother of Fatima bint Hussain, who was present in Karbala and endured captivity.
fullStory:
Umm Ishaq bint Talha ibn Ubaydullah was initially the wife of Imam Hasan Mujtaba (AS). Before his martyrdom, Imam Hasan advised his brother Imam Hussain (AS) to marry her because she was a highly virtuous and believing woman.

The fruit of this marriage was a daughter named 'Fatima bint Hussain' (Fatima Sughra) who married Hasan al-Muthanna in Karbala. Umm Ishaq traveled with her husband's caravan from Medina to Mecca and then to Karbala.

On the day of Ashura, she witnessed the martyrdom of her noble husband, and after the tents were looted, alongside Lady Zaynab (AS) and other women, she was taken as a prisoner of war towards Kufa and Sham.

Umm Ishaq was among the women who heard the Ahl al-Bayt's sermons during captivity, and with patience and steadfastness, defended the sanctuary of Wilayah and brought the message of Karbala's martyrs back to Medina.
quote: (Prayer for the family:) O God! Be his successor among his family and protect them.
